title: 10 best herbal essential oils for jaundice
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for jaundice are natural extracts derived from plants, used to alleviate symptoms associated with this condition. Jaundice is a yellowing of the skin and eyes caused by excess bilirubin in the bloodstream. These oils help by promoting liver function, reducing inflammation, and improving digestion. Examples of herbal essential oils that show promise include turmeric oil, which reduces inflammation, and ginger oil, which stimulates digestive enzymes. By using these oils, individuals with jaundice may experience relief from discomfort, improved appetite, and enhanced overall well-being, allowing them to regain a sense of normalcy in their daily lives.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Barberry essential oils helps with jaundice because of its unique ability to stimulate liver function, promoting the efficient removal of bilirubin from the blood. The oil's bitter compounds, such as berberine, also help to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria that can contribute to jaundice. Additionally, barberry essential oils' anti-inflammatory properties can reduce tissue damage and promote healing in the liver and surrounding areas, ultimately helping to alleviate symptoms associated with jaundice.
remedy_properties: Bilirubin-reducing: Barberry essential oil contains berberine, which helps to reduce bilirubin levels in the blood, thereby alleviating jaundice symptoms. Bilirubin is a yellow pigment that builds up in the blood and skin when the liver is unable to process it properly.

Antioxidant: Barberry essential oil's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to liver cells, which can contribute to jaundice development and exacerbate its symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory: Berberine in Barberry essential oil also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with jaundice, making it easier for the body to recover from this condition.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the barberry plant are rich in berberine, a natural compound with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to alleviate symptoms of jaundice.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of barberry contain a higher concentration of alkaloids, particularly berberine, which has been shown to be effective in reducing bilirubin levels in the blood, common in cases of jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh berberis vulgaris leaves and stems, then dry them in the shade for two weeks.
Use a distillation apparatus or a steam distiller to extract the essential oil from dried berberis vulgaris herbs at a ratio of 20:1.
Distill the mixture for approximately four hours, collecting the essential oil as it separates from the water and plant material.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter or a coffee filter to remove any impurities or particles, then transfer it to a dark glass bottle.
Store the berberis vulgaris essential oil in a cool, dry place for up to six months, shaking the bottle daily to ensure even distribution of the active compounds.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Canada thistle essential oils helps with jaundice because of its remarkable anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and choleretic properties. The oil's ability to reduce inflammation in the liver and gallbladder helps to alleviate symptoms such as yellowing of the skin and eyes, dark urine, and pale stools. Additionally, its stimulating effect on bile production can aid in the excretion of bilirubin, breaking down the buildup that causes jaundice. By using Canada thistle essential oils, individuals may experience relief from the discomfort and distress associated with this condition.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: A flavonoid complex found in Milk Thistle seeds, which has been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, helping to reduce liver damage and inflammation associated with jaundice.

Flavonoids: Milk Thistle extracts contain various flavonoids that have antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body, potentially alleviating jaundice symptoms.

Phenolic acids: Milk Thistle also contains phenolic acids, such as caffeic acid and rosmarinic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may help combat infections associated with jaundice.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Canada thistle contain saponins and other bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat liver and bile duct disorders, making them effective in the treatment of jaundice.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems or rhyzomes of Canada thistle have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as itching and abdominal pain associated with jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cirsium arvense leaves and flowers at dawn on a dry sunny day weighing 500 grams.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm dark place for 7 days or until the moisture level drops to 15%.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a copper still with a condenser coil for 4 hours and collect the essential oil.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper filter and then store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting cap at room temperature.
Use the herbal essential oil within 6 months, adding 20 drops to a carrier oil or applying topically as needed for jaundice relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion essential oils helps with jaundice because of its potent liver-stimulating properties. The oil's bitter compounds, such as sesquiterpenes, have been shown to promote bile flow and reduce bilirubin levels in the blood, alleviating the yellowing of the skin and eyes characteristic of jaundice. Additionally, dandelion essential oils' anti-inflammatory effects help soothe digestive discomforts often associated with jaundice, while its antioxidant properties protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by excessive bilirubin buildup.
remedy_properties: Choleretic properties: Dandelion essential oil stimulates the liver to produce more bile, which helps to break down bilirubin and reduce its concentration in the blood, alleviating jaundice symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in dandelion essential oil help to neutralize free radicals that can damage liver cells and exacerbate jaundice conditions, promoting overall liver health.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that may reduce swelling and pain associated with jaundice, as well as help to soothe digestive issues often present in these patients.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which have been shown to help reduce bilirubin levels in the blood, making them effective in treating jaundice.
Flowers: The flowers contain isobarbatene, a compound that has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers and leaves from organic sources or cultivate them in your garden.
Dry the harvested material in a well-ventilated area or using a dehydrator at low temperature for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried herbal material with water at 100 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to obtain essential oil and hydrosol.
Filter the resulting distillate through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to separate the essential oil from the hydrosol within 2 hours of distillation.

Store the obtained taraxacum officinale essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid at room temperature and use it for skin application and aromatherapy after dilution with carrier oils as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with jaundice because of its potent antioxidant properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. The oil's glycyrrhizin content has been shown to promote liver function and regeneration, helping to eliminate bilirubin and other waste products that can accumulate during jaundice. Additionally, licorice essential oil may also help to soothe and calm digestive issues commonly associated with jaundice, such as nausea and vomiting, promoting overall comfort and relief for affected individuals.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can contribute to jaundice symptoms.

Choleretic and cholagogue effects: Licorice root stimulates the production of bile and helps facilitate its flow from the liver through the small intestine, which can alleviate symptoms of jaundice such as yellowing of the skin and eyes.

Hepatoprotective effects: Licorice root's flavonoids and saponins have been shown to protect the liver against damage caused by toxic substances, viruses, or other environmental stressors, which may help prevent or reduce the severity of jaundice.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain a high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and help reduce bilirubin levels in the blood.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the licorice plant are rich in saponins, which can help stimulate digestion and alleviate symptoms of jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots weighing 500 grams, ensuring proper cleanliness.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or until crispy texture achieved.
Steam distill the dried roots using 1000 milliliters of water at atmospheric pressure for 4 hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil using a coffee filter paper, yielding 20 milliliters of final product.
Store the extracted essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, ensuring airtight seal within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with jaundice because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver. Jaundice is often caused by a blockage in the bile ducts, leading to an accumulation of bilirubin in the blood. Chamomile oil's soothing and calming effects can help to relax the muscles and reduce spasms in the bile ducts, allowing for smoother flow and reduced bilirubin buildup. This can help to alleviate symptoms such as yellowing skin and eyes, and promote overall liver health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds may help soothe and calm irritated skin, which can be a common symptom in patients with jaundice.

Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants may help protect the liver from further damage and oxidative stress, which could potentially aid in the treatment of underlying liver conditions causing jaundice.
Sedative and anti-anxiety effects: Chamomile's calming effects can help alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation, which is essential for patients experiencing discomfort or distress due to jaundice.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant contain a high concentration of apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and promote liver function.
Barks: The barks of the chamomile plant have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including jaundice, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn and dry them in a well-ventilated area for 7 days.
Mix one cup of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Infuse the mixture in a glass jar with a lid, storing it in a dark place at room temperature for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids, retaining the infused carrier oil that contains the essential oils.
Transfer the infused carrier oil to a dark glass bottle, labeling and dating it to use within 6 months for its therapeutic properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort essential oils helps with jaundice because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The oil contains a number of bioactive compounds, including hyperforin and hypericin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve liver function. By reducing oxidative stress and promoting healthy liver cells, St John's wort essential oil may help to alleviate the yellowing of the skin and eyes associated with jaundice, as well as support overall liver health and function.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: St. John's Wort's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with liver damage, which is often the root cause of jaundice.
Choleretic effect: The herb has been shown to stimulate bile production and improve liver function, which can help alleviate symptoms of jaundice such as yellowing of the skin and eyes (jaundice).

Anti-inflammatory activity: St. John's Wort's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the liver and pancreas, which is often present in cases of viral hepatitis, a common cause of jaundice.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their bitter and pungent properties, which help to stimulate liver function and improve digestion, aiding in the treatment of jaundice.

Flowers: The flowers contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making them effective in reducing liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers at dawn on a sunny day in summer.
Dry the harvested flowers using a food dehydrator or air circulation for 24 hours.
Combine 200 grams of dried st john's wort flowers with 400 milliliters of carrier oil in a dark glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for solvent extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container and discard the solids after 2 hours preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with jaundice because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It has been traditionally used to reduce bilirubin levels in the blood, which is often elevated in cases of jaundice. The menthol content in peppermint oil also helps to improve digestion and relieve nausea, common symptoms associated with jaundice. By promoting liver function and reducing inflammation, peppermint essential oils may help alleviate the yellowing of the skin and eyes characteristic of jaundice, providing relief and comfort to those affected.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Peppermint essential oil's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can alleviate jaundice symptoms such as yellowing of the skin and eyes.

Choleretic properties: The choleretic properties of peppermint essential oil stimulate the production of bile in the liver, which can help to improve digestion, reduce bilirubin levels, and alleviate jaundice symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the liver and digestive tract, which can contribute to jaundice by reducing blood flow and increasing bilirubin production.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain a high concentration of menthol, which has been shown to help reduce bile duct spasms and improve liver function in individuals with jaundice.
Leaves: Fresh peppermint leaves have a calming effect on the digestive system, which can help alleviate symptoms such as nausea and vomiting commonly associated with jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves in abundance from your garden or a local farmer's market.
Steam-distill the peppermint leaves using a still and collect the resulting essential oil in a glass container.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter or coffee filter to remove any impurities or debris.
Store the filtered essential oil in an amber glass bottle, away from direct sunlight and heat sources for six months.
Dilute the peppermint essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a 1:3 ratio before topical application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle essential oils helps with jaundice because it contains silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that has been shown to have a protective effect on the liver. Jaundice is often caused by liver damage or inflammation, and milk thistle's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce this inflammation, promoting healthy liver function. Additionally, silymarin has been found to increase the production of liver enzymes responsible for detoxification, helping to remove bilirubin from the blood and alleviate jaundiced skin and eyes.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Milk thistle extract contains a flavonoid complex called silymarin, which has been shown to protect liver cells from damage and promote liver regeneration. This property can be beneficial in reducing the severity of jaundice caused by liver inflammation or injury.
Antioxidant activity: Milk thistle extract has antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate liver damage and inflammation associated with jaundice.

Hepatoprotective effects: The active compounds in milk thistle extract have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, meaning they help protect the liver from injury and inflammation, which can be beneficial in managing jaundice caused by liver disease or toxicity.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of silymarin, a flavonoid complex that helps reduce liver inflammation and protect against oxidative stress associated with jaundice.
Seeds: Milk thistle seeds are rich in saponins, which have been shown to stimulate the regeneration of liver cells and improve liver function, making them effective in treating jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum leaves and flowers at dawn on a sunny day and dry them thoroughly in shade for 2 hours.
Crush dried silybum marianum leaves and flowers into fine powder using mortar and pestle and sift through mesh screen.
Mix 10 grams of powdered silybum marianum with 100 milliliters of high-proof industrial ethanol and store mixture at room temperature for 24 hours.
Filter the mixture through filter paper to separate essential oil from plant material and discard plant material.
Transfer filtered essential oil into dark glass bottle and store in refrigerator at 40°f for up to 6 months while shaking periodically every week.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood essential oils helps with jaundice because of its unique properties that target the liver's function. Jaundice occurs when there is an excessive buildup of bilirubin in the blood, which can lead to yellowing of the skin and eyes. Wormwood oil contains sesquiterpenoids that stimulate the liver's natural detoxification processes, increasing the removal of bilirubin from the bloodstream. This helps to reduce jaundice symptoms and promote overall liver health. Additionally, wormwood oil's anti-inflammatory properties may also help alleviate discomfort associated with jaundice.
remedy_properties: Choleretic and cholagogue properties: Wormwood's bitter compounds stimulate the liver and gallbladder to release bile, which can help relieve symptoms of jaundice such as yellowing of the skin and eyes.
Antimicrobial activity: Wormwood has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which may help combat infections that can contribute to the development of jaundice.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the liver and other tissues, which can alleviate symptoms of jaundice such as abdominal pain and nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, the roots of wormwood have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce liver inflammation associated with jaundice.
Buds: Containing a high concentration of camphor, the buds of wormwood are used to make essential oils that can help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh artemisia absinthium leaves and stems in early morning when dew is still present on the plants.
Cleanly chop 500 grams of harvested plant material into small pieces for efficient steam distillation.
Combine chopped plant material with 1000 milliliters of distilled water in a large stainless steel or glass vessel.
Steam distill the plant material mixture at 100 to 105 degrees celsius for 2 to 3 hours using a clevenger apparatus.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a paper coffee filter or cotton gauze into a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose essential oils helps with jaundice because of its unique properties that promote liver health and detoxification. The oil's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds help to reduce bilirubin levels in the blood, alleviating symptoms such as yellowing of the skin and eyes. Additionally, damask rose essential oils can stimulate bile production, improving digestion and eliminating toxins from the body. As a natural remedy, it offers a gentle yet effective solution for managing jaundice symptoms and promoting overall health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Damask rose essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with jaundice, which can occur due to inflammation in the liver or bile ducts.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of damask rose essential oil help protect the liver from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of jaundice.

Choleretic: Damask rose essential oil has choleretic properties, which means it helps stimulate the production and secretion of bile in the liver, helping to improve digestion and reduce symptoms of jaundice such as yellowing of the skin and eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the damask rose plant are used due to their ability to stimulate liver function and promote detoxification, which can help alleviate symptoms of jaundice.
Seeds: Damask rose seeds contain potent antioxidant compounds that can help protect against liver damage and inflammation caused by jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rose petals and wash them gently with lukewarm water and mild soap to remove dirt and debris.
Steam distill the rose petals in a stainless steel apparatus at atmospheric pressure for 4-6 hours to release essential oil.
Collect the steam-distilled essential oil from the condenser coil and filter it through a paper coffee filter to remove impurities.
Transfer the filtered essential oil into dark glass bottles and store them in a cool, dry place for 24-48 hours to allow sedimentation.
Decant the clear rosa damascena essential oil from the sediments and use it as needed or dilute with carrier oils for topical application.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jaundice with herbal essential oils is by using a combination of Chamomile and Lemon balm essential oils. Mix equal parts of both oils and apply topically to the skin in gentle circular motions. This blend helps reduce inflammation and promotes liver function, aiding in the breakdown and excretion of bilirubin. Additionally, inhaling the aromas can also help soothe digestive issues often associated with jaundice. Consult a healthcare professional before use, especially if pregnant or breastfeeding.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaundice that are treated with herbal essential oils are hepatitis, liver congestion, and gallbladder issues. Essential oils such as Dandelion, Milk Thistle, and Turmeric have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce liver inflammation and improve bile flow. These oils can also be used topically or in a diffuser to promote liver detoxification and overall liver health. Additionally, essential oils like Lemon and Ginger have been traditionally used to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of gallbladder issues.
